summary: Added a minimal Metal renderer skeleton for the macOS app shell plus a core render timing probe that clears a BGRA8 render target to a validation-red test pattern, samples the rendered pixel, and writes stable JSON evidence from Xcode tests.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Renderer path builds and tests on macOS
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/xcodebuild.log records a passing Xcode run; evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/macos-check.log records the final passing full macOS gate.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A visible frame or test pattern is presented
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: apple/Sources/MadobeMac/MetalTestPatternView.swift provides the MTKView-backed test pattern view and apple/Sources/MadobeMac/MadobeMacApp.swift wires it into the app shell. The hardware render report is offscreen render-target evidence, not display/drawable presentation evidence.

evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/render-report.json records renderer metal-clear-test-pattern, commandBufferStatus completed, sampledPixelBGRA8 [0,0,255,255], and renderDurationNanoseconds. Its legacy presentedTestPattern field means offscreen test-pattern validation, not display presentation.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Screenshot evidence is saved when practical
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/notes.md records why no GUI screenshot was practical in this noninteractive run; the MTKView app path and hardware render report provide practical validation evidence.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No streaming latency claim is made until cross-device validation
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m3-metal-renderer-skeleton/notes.md explicitly limits the node to local Metal render skeleton/timing evidence and makes no streaming or cross-device latency claim.
